description: This figure presents a schematic roadmap illustrating the development pathway of Fe-Mn-C-Al low-density steel, structured as a sequential three-stage process flow connected by a dashed pathway. The first stage, "Alloyings," focuses on controlling steel density, precipitation behavior, and strengthening mechanisms through compositional design with Mn, Al, and C additions. The second stage, "Annealing & aging treatments," involves critical thermal processing steps to induce phase changes and precipitation growth (particularly κ-carbide formation). The final stage yields the target "Low density steel" exhibiting four key performance attributes: lower density, higher strength, wear resistance, and low material cost. This visualization encapsulates the processing-microstructure-property paradigm central to the review, demonstrating how specific alloying strategies and controlled heat treatments translate Fe-Mn-C-Al alloys into lightweight structural materials with superior mechanical performance for automotive and aerospace applications.
